rulings.Case Title: TELEFLORA VS. ROSSI & ROVETTI FLOWERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Of Plaintiffs Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Dismissal
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 15, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF TELEFLORA'S Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Dismissal. Denied. Plaintiff should file a motion to enforce the settlement agreement. The dismissal does not have to be set aside in order for the Court to enforce the settlement agreement. (See Wackeen v. Malis (2002) 97 Cal.App.4th 429, 439; Storper v. Dillick 2013 WL 2378553.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SRB)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN SALSER VS. SYNERGY PROJECT MANAGEMENT, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Deft'S Motion To Compel Independent Mental Examination
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 15, 2015, Line 7, Defendants Synergy Project Management, Inc. and Scott Alan Jones' Motion To Compel Independent Mental Examination. The motion to compel independent mental examination of Plaintiff is granted. Further, Defendant's request that only Plaintiff attend the examination, without presence of counsel, is granted. (See Edwards vs. Sup. Ct. (1976) 16 Cal 3d 905, 910.) Defendant has shown good cause exists for an independent mental examination because Plaintiff is claiming he suffers from PTSD as a result of the accident at issue in this case. A psychological examination is necessary for Defendant's expert to reach an accurate understanding of Plaintiff's condition. (See CCP Sec. 2032.210.) The Court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SRB)

rulings.Case Title: CUI XIA RONG VS. ZHI YAN PAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Persons Most Qualified At Shl Law Group And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday May 15, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE, ANNA MAU'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of Persons Most Qualified At Shl Law Group And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to compel the deposition of Person Most Qualified at SHL Law Group is GRANTED. The witness previously produced as the PMQ was not properly designated. The defendants' request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $1,000, payable within fifteen (15) days of entry of order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KATHRYN LOWRIE VS. UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion By Defendant United Parcel Service, Inc. For Pain Management Examination Of Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 15, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC.'S Motion For Pain Management Examination Of Plaintiff. The motion is granted. Defendant has presented good cause for a pain management examination. See Liao Decl., 2:10-12. The fact that the recommendation by Dr. Kim does not appear in her written report does not mean that Dr. Kim did not make the recommendation. The parties are to meet and confer on the date and scope of the examination and submit a proposed order to contestdept302tr@sftc.org by 5pm in Word format. The Court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA MARIE REKOW VS. MICHAEL PATRICK FAY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 15, 2015, Line 10, PLAINTIFF CHRISTINA REKOW'S Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint. Plaintiff's motion for leave to amend complaint is granted. Judicial policy favors resolution of all disputed matters between the parties in the same lawsuit. The court's discretion will be exercised liberally to permit amendment of the pleadings. [See Nestle v. Santa Monica (1972) 6 C3d 920, 939; Mabie v. Hyatt (1998) 61 CA4th 581, 596; Howard v. County of San Diego (2010) 184 CA4th 1422, 1428.] In tort cases, Plaintiff may seek punitive damages for "oppression, fraud or malice" by Defendant. [See Civ.C. Sec. 3294(a).] Here, a trier of fact could find Defendant Padover's actions rise to the level of "malice" and may find punitive damages are appropriate. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SRB)
